description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block is solid and has a dark, almost black coloration with lighter gray highlights on the grooved surfaces, suggesting a polished or treated stone material. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The overall appearance is robust and utilitarian, suitable for use as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments. The design is functional, likely intended to prevent passage or to serve as a heavy-duty separator in public spaces.
description: A solid rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, alternating between dark and light stone. The sides are smooth and the overall look is robust and utilitarian.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped, grooved top surface with the grooves running horizontally across the block.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the full width of the grooved pattern across the block.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the vertical grooves and the contrast between the raised and recessed areas.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a traffic or pedestrian barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or directly on a leveled ground base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
